assertion_results.text: Investigation identifies two independent root causes: (1) convention reference formatter using wrong case transform, (2) task creation using Feature issue type ID instead of Task (Steps 2-4)
assertion_results.evidence: investigation.md Step 4 Root Cause Analysis explicitly identifies two root causes: Root Cause 1 'Convention reference formatter uses wrong case transform (kebab-case instead of preserving title case)' in shared/convention-utils.md, and Root Cause 2 'Task creation uses Feature issue type ID (10142) instead of Task issue type ID (10050)' in plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a. The summary table confirms 'two independent root causes in different modules affecting different execution stages.' Steps 2-3 trace both issues through their respective code paths.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard is triggered because the two root causes are in different modules/code paths (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: investigation.md concludes with 'These are two independent root causes in different modules affecting different execution stages. Fixing one does not fix the other, and neither depends on the other. This triggers the Decomposition Guard (Step 6).' The decomposition-guard.md file exists and is explicitly titled 'Decomposition Guard — ACME-502', confirming the guard was triggered. It states 'These two problems are caused by independent code paths in different modules'.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard presents both issues with their affected files/modules (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md presents both issues with their affected files/modules: Issue 1 identifies 'the convention reference formatter in shared/convention-utils.md' affecting 'task description generation during Step 5 of plan-feature', and Issue 2 identifies 'the task creation logic in plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a' using 'the Feature issue type ID (10142) from Jira Configuration instead of the Task issue type ID (10050)'. The module breakdown explicitly lists 'Issue 1 is in the shared convention formatting utilities (shared/convention-utils.md)' and 'Issue 2 is in the plan-feature task creation logic (plan-feature/SKILL.md Step 6a)'.

assertion_results.text: Decomposition Guard offers the user two options: Proceed with single Task or Split into separate Bugs (Step 6)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md presents exactly two options: '1. **Proceed** -- create a single Task covering all fixes' and '2. **Split** -- I recommend creating separate Bugs for each independent issue, then triaging each one individually'. This matches the required pattern of offering Proceed with single Task or Split into separate Bugs.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT silently create a single Task bundling both fixes — it waits for user input (Step 6 stop rule)
assertion_results.evidence: decomposition-guard.md ends with 'Choose (1/2):' — an explicit prompt waiting for user input. No task creation output files exist in the outputs directory (only investigation.md and decomposition-guard.md are present). The skill stopped at the Decomposition Guard and did not proceed to create any Jira tasks.
